phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest US yield and Fed policy data relevant to XAUUSD (Gold). Fetch and report: - US 10-Year Nominal Treasury Yield (exact %) - US 10-Year TIPS Yield — this is the Real Yield (inflation-adjusted, exact %) - Real Yield Direction: is the real yield rising or falling vs yesterday? (Rising real yields = bearish for Gold; Falling real yields = bullish for Gold) - DXY (US Dollar Index): exact current level and today's directional move (Up/Down X%). Note: a weakening DXY is bullish for Gold and vice versa. - Fed current policy stance: Hawkish / Neutral / Dovish - CME FedWatch: probability of a rate cut at the NEXT Fed meeting (exact %) - Fed year-end Terminal Rate expectation (from CME FedWatch year-end strip or analyst consensus — exact %) - Any Fed speaker comments in the last 48 hours that could impact Gold - Core Logic: summarise whether the Real Yield + DXY combination is currently net Bullish, Bearish, or Neutral for Gold ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - US 10Y Nominal Yield: [%] | US 10Y TIPS Real Yield: [%] | Real Yield Trend: [Rising/Falling] - DXY: [level] | Today's Move: [Up/Down X%] | Gold Correlation: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Neutral/Dovish] | Next Meeting Cut Probability: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - Key Fed Speaker Note: [most impactful recent comment, or None] - Real Yield + DXY Combined Signal: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral for Gold] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - REAL YIELDS & FED POLICY] - US 10Y Nominal: [%] | US 10Y TIPS Real Yield: [%] | Trend: [Rising/Falling] - DXY: [level] | Move: [direction %] | DXY Gold Signal: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Neutral/Dovish] | Next Cut Probability: [%] | Year-End Terminal: [%] - Key Fed Speaker: [comment or None] - Core Logic: [why this Real Yield + DXY combo is bullish/bearish/neutral for Gold today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report: - Active war zones and conflict escalations in the last 48 hours (e.g., Russia-Ukraine, Middle East / Israel-Gaza, Taiwan Strait, South China Sea tensions) — rate each as: Active Escalation / Stable / De-escalating - Middle East headline risk: any specific events or threats in the last 24 hours? - VIX (CBOE Volatility Index): exact current level. Low VIX + calm geopolitics + equity rally = Risk-On = BEARISH for Gold. - Overall Safe-Haven demand rating for Gold today: [Very High / High / Moderate / Low / Very Low] ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Active Conflict Status: [summary of top 2 active zones + escalation level] - Middle East Headline Risk: [specific event or Calm] - VIX: [level] | Classification: [Low/Elevated/High/Extreme Fear] - S&P 500: [direction %] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On / Risk-Off / Neutral] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Financial System Stress: [event or None] - Safe-Haven Demand Rating: [Very High / High / Moderate / Low / Very Low] - Geopolitical Gold Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - GEOPOLITICAL RISK & SAFE-HAVEN] - Top Conflict Zone: [name + escalation status] - Middle East Risk: [event or Calm] - VIX: [level] | Classification: [Low/Elevated/High/Extreme Fear] - S&P 500: [direction %] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off/Neutral] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Financial System Stress: [event or None] - Safe-Haven Demand Rating: [Very High / High / Moderate / Low / Very Low] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [whether current fear/risk environment is pushing capital into Gold as safe haven] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear XAUUSD (Gold) institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Real Yields & Fed Policy: [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Inflation & Employment Data: [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Geopolitical Risk & Safe-Haven: [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Institutional & Central Bank Demand: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Sentiment & Positioning (COT): [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Remember the core Gold logic: Falling real yields + weak DXY + geopolitical fear + strong CB demand = Bullish for Gold. Rising real yields + strong DXY + risk-on + CB pausing = Bearish for Gold.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this Gold bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[XAUUSD MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
```

phase8_blog_html: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Act as an expert Gold Market Analyst and Financial Blogger. Using the research data below, write a comprehensive, highly detailed blog post explaining today's XAUUSD (Gold) fundamental bias so anyone reading it can fully understand the market conditions driving Gold prices.
